title: Fix bugprone-exception-escape in Cyber RT base coroutine scheduler

title: Fix bugprone-branch-clone in Cyber RT scheduler dispatch paths
note: Rewrite of CCCL bugprone-branch-clone. CUB→scheduler: remove duplicated branch logic in scheduler task-dispatch paths identified by clang-tidy bugprone-branch-clone.

title: Cyber RT base: Add compile-time message-format string verify tests
note: Rewrite of CCCL libcudacxx: Add compile-time format string verify tests. libcu++→base: implement static_assert-based compile-time format-string verification for Cyber RT base message templates.

title: Implement PoC for node-resident message-count in data reduce for run-to-run and non-deterministic pub/sub paths
note: Rewrite of CCCL device-resident problem size in device reduce. thrust→data: PoC where the subscriber node holds message-count state for run-to-run and non-guaranteed determinism in Cyber RT data pipeline reduce operations.

title: Strip zero-width or unprintable unicode characters from Cyber RT tools log sanitizer
note: Rewrite of CCCL Strip zero-width or unprintable unicode characters. infra→tools: add unicode sanitization pass in the Cyber RT tools/log-sanitizer to strip zero-width and unprintable characters from pub/sub message payloads.

title: Warpspeed run-to-run deterministic message scan for Cyber RT scheduler using atomic global sequence counter
note: Rewrite of CCCL warpspeed run_to_run deterministic scan for SM90 using atomic global counter. CUB→scheduler: implement run-to-run deterministic message-scan in Cyber RT scheduler using an atomic global sequence counter for the pub/sub loop.

title: [data] Use Cyber RT Runtime in remaining CUDA set-intersection subscriber tests
note: Rewrite of CCCL [thrust] Use CCCL Runtime in remaining CUDA set-intersection tests. thrust→data: migrate remaining set-intersection subscriber tests to use the Cyber RT Runtime APIs in the data module.

title: [data] Use Cyber RT Runtime in CUDA extrema subscriber tests
note: Rewrite of CCCL [thrust] Use CCCL Runtime in CUDA extrema tests. thrust→data: migrate CUDA extrema subscriber tests to Cyber RT Runtime in the data module pub/sub-loop test suite.

title: `cyber::isclose()` — approximate equality helper for Cyber RT message comparisons
note: Rewrite of CCCL cuda::isclose(). libcu++→base: implement cyber::isclose() in the Cyber RT base module for approximate floating-point equality checks in pub/sub message validation and test assertions.
